text: Entire ecosystems thrive in total darkness — powered by chemistry, not sunlight. Deep sea vents, like black smokers, erupt with superheated water and minerals. Here, life forms like tube worms and giant clams flourish without sunlight. They rely on chemosynthesis — converting chemicals from the vents into energy. But what about the origin of life itself? This remains a hot topic in science. Could these vents hold secrets about how life began on Earth? Research continues. Dive deeper into ocean mysteries. Like and follow for more!
